title: Top 10 Highest Peaks in the Alps | All Above 4000 Meters - Alpenwild
content: - Self-Guided Tour du Mont Blanc. - Self-Guided Best of Swiss Alps. - Self-Guided FAQ - Tour du Mont Blanc. The Alp's top ten tallest peaks are almost all located in Switzerland with the exception of Mont Blanc being in France and Italy, and both Monte Rosa and Liskamm being shared by Italy and Switzerland. Mont Blanc is the highest peak in the Alps and in Europe, reaching a lofty 4,804 meters (15,774 ft) above sea level. The Tour de Mont Rosa is a difficult trek that circles Monte Rosa, crossing through both the Swiss and Italian Alps. Liskamm reaches 4,527 meters (14,852 ft) above sea level, and is located in the Pennine Alps on the border of Switzerland and Italy. The Weisshorn, located near the Matterhorn in the Pennine Alps, has an elevation of 4,506 meters (14,783 ft). Dent Blanche is one of the most difficult climbs of the 4,000+ meter peaks in the Alps.

title: 10 Highest Mountain In The Alps - Climbing Kilimanjaro
content: The Alps, one of Europe’s most breathtaking mountain ranges, offers a vast array of outdoor adventures for all types of travelers. With eight countries spanning its borders, the Alps boast a diverse terrain and some of the highest peaks in the world. Let’s take a closer look at the top ten tallest peaks in the Alps, located mostly in Switzerland with a few shared by other countries. Mont-Blanc- Highest-Mountain-in-the-alps. Mont Blanc is the highest peak in the Alps at an impressive  4,804 meters (15,774 ft) above sea level. Towering over the Alps at an impressive 4,804 meters (15,774 ft), Mont Blanc is the highest peak in the Alps and also the second highest peak in all of Europe. The Monte Rosa massif, located on the border between Switzerland and Italy, is home to the second tallest peak in the Alps, the Dufourspitze at 4,634 meters (15,203 ft). This peak, located between the Matterhorn and the Weisshorn, offers a variety of straightforward routes for climbers and provides breathtaking views from the Gornergrat.
